content: When a refine/inventory ticket asks to compare N surfaces against a new analytical lens (role-card shape, loop-moves, anatomy gaps), scope the lens to the surfaces where it carries semantic meaning (e.g. actual LLM prompts) rather than running it across every deterministic-script/config row too — the latter produces mostly-null cells and quadruples the document for no signal.

Do not author full new role-card/persona content as a side effect of a size:S inventory ticket — an inventory ticket's job is to identify candidates (source prompts + one-line rationale) and defer authoring to a follow-up implementation issue, especially when the pattern being adopted (e.g. agency-agents role cards) has zero prior references in the repo.

When extracting a reviewer/persona prompt that is consumed by more than one phase command (e.g. conformance-reviewer-prompt.md, used by both dark-factory-plan.md's Phase 3.5 and the dedicated conformance gate) into a real Claude Code Skill, do not nest it inside another phase-specific skill (e.g. 'refinement') just because an earlier inventory-based worked example (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-10-dark-factory-claude-skills-design.md §2) or a pre-policy command reference (commands/dark-factory-code-review.md, added in #218 before the skills policy existed) happened to assume that location. Give cross-cutting reviewer personas their own dedicated bare-capability-noun skill (e.g. .claude/skills/conformance/, .claude/skills/code-review/) per the #42 naming convention, since the two follow-up tickets (#43 refine/plan, #44 conformance/code-review) already split the five refinement-skills/ prompts by phase-pairing, not into one skill.

When migrating a baked /opt/refinement-skills/<x>-prompt.md file to a clone-live .claude/skills/<name>/ path, never hard-cut-over the command's read step — resolve clone-live-first with baked-path fallback, mirroring entrypoint.sh:40's config.yaml precedent (/.claude/skills/refinement/config.yaml, then /opt/refinement-skills/config.yaml). Update dark-factory/scripts/context_budget.py's _SKILL_PROMPT_FILES/_SKILL_PROMPT_DIR probe in the same lockstep, per-file rather than via the single directory join, or the skill_prompts context-budget section silently measures a stale or missing file. commands/dark-factory-code-review.md already reads .claude/skills/refinement/code-review-reviewer-prompt.md today (added in #218) with no fallback and no file present at that path — a live example of the silent-gap failure mode this causes, masked because code_review.fail_open defaults true.
